description: Only explicitly prefixed env variables are exposed to client code by Vite conventions.
body: Use when frontend config or API URL changes. Do not expose secrets by moving server env into client bundle. Follow existing env prefix and runtime config conventions, especially Nuxt runtimeConfig. Verify build/dev env behavior. Risk boundary: secret leakage to browser bundle. Use when Vue Frontend Engineering work touches visible UI behavior, browser console output, DOM state, assets, viewport behavior, and interaction evidence. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include browser smoke, component tests, viewport screenshots, console/network checks, and accessibility assertions. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves visual regressions, inaccessible interaction, hydration mismatch, and browser-only failures, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
